dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal describes two pattern types: (1) Reversal candle: success when close < prev_low (price drops below previous candle low), failure when close > prev_high. (2) Continuation candle: success when breakout follows entry direction, failure when it reverses. Modeled as two opposing entry conditions (long reversal vs short reversal) with reciprocal exit conditions. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as pattern type determines long vs short. Author provided 69% bull success rate but no other specific parameters, so reasonable defaults applied (2% stop_loss, 5-day hold). Sample size: 103 candles.

(2) Continuation candle: success when breakout follows entry direction, failure when it reverses. Modeled as two opposing entry conditions (long reversal vs short reversal) with reciprocal exit conditions. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as pattern type determines long vs short. Author provided 69% bull success rate but no other specific parameters, so reasonable defaults applied (2% stop_loss, 5-day hold). Sample size: 103 candles.

source_rule_text: Candlestick Reversal/Continuation Pattern: A reversal candle should cause price to drop (close below previous low = success, close above preceding candle top = failure). A continuation candle should break out in the same direction as entry (upward entry should break out up = success, downward breakout = failure).
